items.title: Two dead in car crash in Tasmania's south

children.content: Two vehicles collided at Old Beach in Tasmania's south earlier today. The two occupants of one of the vehicles have died at the scene, while the other driver is uninjured.

gallery.altText: Several police cars and an ambulance seen on a stretch of road that turns a corner, with a river on one side.

thumbnail.altText: Several police cars and an ambulance seen on a stretch of road that turns a corner, with a river on one side.

items.title: Hobart's Southern Outlet widening project blows out again

children.content: The cost of widening Hobart's Southern Outlet has blown out by another $32 million, with completion of the northern section of the project now estimated at $95 million.

image.altText: Southern Outlet Hobart

items.title: Logging company gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria

children.content: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

image.altText: A semi-trailer loaded with logs in a traffic lane approaching a port. The large Spirit of Tasmania ship is in front of it.

items.title: Poultry producers warn bird flu would be 'catastrophic for Tasmania'

children.content: Tasmanian poultry producers are ramping up biosecurity measures, as they prepare for the arrival of a deadly strain of bird flu. The state government has stressed that it's well prepared, but says euthanasia is "definitely possible" if there's an outbreak in commercial poultry.

gallery.altText: Brad stands in front of his farm gate, which has a biosecurity sign on it. He crosses his arms and looks at the camera.
About 30 rusty red coloured chooks peck the grass in front of farming couple Phil and Angie Glover on their Tasmanian farm
Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

thumbnail.altText: Brad stands in front of his farm gate, which has a biosecurity sign on it. He crosses his arms and looks at the camera.
About 30 rusty red coloured chooks peck the grass in front of farming couple Phil and Angie Glover on their Tasmanian farm
Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

items.title: Union launches bid to save Boag's brewery from closure

children.content: The United Workers Union has launched a petition calling on Boag's parent company Lion Australia to sell rather than close its Launceston brewery. The union has also written to Premier Jeremy Rockliff urging him to intervene.

image.altText: red brick brewery with boags signage and a big green beer can

items.title: Christian Brothers 'squib' on abuse compensation, lawyer says

children.content: The Catholic order says it will sell off its remaining assets to fund abuse compensation, but the money raised is expected to fall short of what is owed to victims.

image.altText: Cross on top of a church

items.title: 'We lost our son': India's medical exams scandal leads to tragedy and uproar

children.content: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

gallery.altText: Four women in floral veils have their heads bowed, holding a photo of a young man wearing glasses.

thumbnail.altText: Four women in floral veils have their heads bowed, holding a photo of a young man wearing glasses.

items.title: 'Ridiculous and disrespectful' inquiry into NDIS cuts extended

children.content: Disability advocates welcome an extension to a Senate inquiry probing a bill paving the way for the biggest-ever cuts to the NDIS.

image.altText: A middle-aged white man sitting in a wheelchair. He is wearing glasses and a dark and scarf beanie

items.title: 'They've been caught out': KPMG fallout continues with resignations

children.content: KPMG chair Martin Sheppard and two audit partners are leaving the accounting and consulting giant in the wake of a scandal over misuse of confidential client information and the alleged mistreatment of a whistleblower.

image.altText: KPMG

items.title: Newly discovered spider uses 'ballista' web to capture prey

children.content: A bizarre species of spider from the rainforests of Far North Queensland springs a nasty surprise.

image.altText: Close up vision of a small spider tangling above an ant on a leaf

gallery.altText: A close up of a leaf an ant approaches a cone of spider web while a spider hovers above

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a leaf an ant approaches a cone of spider web while a spider hovers above

items.title: Video of nurse allegedly threatening Israelis excluded from court case

children.content: Lawyers argued the viral video clip from an online chat with an Israeli influencer breached NSW laws prohibiting recording private conversations without consent.

image.altText: Ahmad Rashad Nadir and Sarah Abu Lebdeh both wearing sunglasses.

items.title: NT Labor leader defends office culture in 'unique' political workplace

children.content: Northern Territory Opposition Leader Selena Uibo has defended her handling of workplace misconduct allegations in her office, saying it is a "highly-charged political environment that is unique and unlike most workplaces".

image.altText: A woman, looking serious, standing in front of a few media microphones at an outdoor press conference.

items.title: Can Australia still make the knockout rounds of the World Cup?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is well underway and it's starting to become clear which teams are progressing through to the next round of competition. But, Australia's fate still isn't sealed.
Australia currently sitting second in its group with one win against Türkiye and one loss against the United States.
We break down how the new qualifying process works, what's changed, and what the Socceroos need from their match on Friday against Paraguay.

image.altText: Footballer in yellow jersey on blue background smiles with arms raised. Text: A guide to the World Cup knockout & Australia's ch

gallery.altText: Footballer in yellow jersey on blue background smiles with arms raised. Text: A guide to the World Cup knockout & Australia's ch

thumbnail.altText: Footballer in yellow jersey on blue background smiles with arms raised. Text: A guide to the World Cup knockout & Australia's ch

items.title: New Mariners owner snubs women's side, coach told services not needed

children.content: A new owner has been confirmed for struggling A-League club the Central Coast Mariners, but it has only bought the men's team and the club's academy, leaving the future of the women's team in doubt.

image.altText: A group of soccer players sitting on a field with a trophy. They are all smiling

items.title: Students protest AI amid growing national concerns

children.content: As Australia tracks toward becoming a global leader in artificial intelligence infrastructure, a group of high school students is demanding stronger regulation.

image.altText: Young woman holding hand painted sign reading "school strike for AI regulation" more students and signs behind her

items.title: 'Look at us go!': First Nations actors celebrate Logies nods

children.content: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

gallery.altText: LIla as Evonne takes a swing at a tennise ball during a match.

thumbnail.altText: LIla as Evonne takes a swing at a tennise ball during a match.

avatar.alt: Woman with neat, short hair smiles warmly for professional head portrait. Blurred trees are in the background

items.title: 'Like looking into a mirror': I met my siblings at the age of 35

children.content: Cheryl van Wageningen was shocked to receive a letter revealing that not only was she Indigenous, but she had a brother and a sister.

image.altText: An old photo of two women sitting next to each other on a brick wall wearing dresses

items.title: You won't see Hugh Jackman the same after The Death of Robin Hood

children.content: Plus Toy Story 5 trots out the same old games, Australian horror scores another winner and Colony uses zombies to ring the warning bell about AI.

image.altText: Hugh Jackman in The Death of Robin Hood

items.title: Alessandro Circati chose Australia over Italy. His dad approves

children.content: Alessandro Circati's decision to play for the Socceroos raised eyebrows within his family in Italy, but his father says they now realise that, deep down, he's not Italian.

articles.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

children.content: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

image.altText: Lionel Messi waves to the crowd as he walks off the field after a game

articles.title: Quick hits: Wild weather, Messi magic and Merlin meets the president

children.content: Some wild weather brings the World Cup to a standstill, but not before Lionel Messi lights it up to rewrite the record books. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Merlin the duck, Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe

articles.title: Haaland, Mbappe and Messi make it another magic World Cup day

children.content: Lionel Messi breaks a World Cup scoring record, Kylian Mbappe and France put the foot down against Iraq after big weather delay, Erling Haaland strikes again for Norway and Jordan is eliminated.

image.altText: Erling Haaland holds his arms skyward in celebration

gallery.altText: Alessandro Circati celebrates after Socceroos' win over Türkiye at FIFA World Cup.

thumbnail.altText: Alessandro Circati celebrates after Socceroos' win over Türkiye at FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Who wins and who loses in the NSW budget

children.content: With billions for health but limited funds for the state's regions, here are the 2026 NSW budget's winners and losers.

image.altText: A graphic of the Australia map in blue with NSW bolded, and gold coins around the map.

items.title: The winners and losers in Queensland's state budget

children.content: The Queensland treasurer has handed down the state budget. Take a look at what it includes and who benefits.

image.altText: a graphic of a map of queensland with gold coins

items.title: Photo gives clues to missing man's final hours before disappearance

children.content: A previously unreleased image answers one question in the case of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan, but many others remain.

image.altText: Jack McLennan jumps into Barambah Creek hours before he vanished.

items.title: Church youth leader sentenced for sexually assaulting 13-year-old

children.content: A man who assaulted a girl in his car has dodged a lengthy jail term, with a judge saying there were "exceptional circumstances" that warranted a discounted sentence.

image.altText: Joshua Emonson poses for a photo with four other people

items.title: Perfect dupes of ABC articles are fuelling an industrial-scale scam

children.content: A $350 million scam is targeting Australians using AI-generated images of ABC journalists and politicians.

gallery.altText: An illustration of a phone inside a bear trap

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of a phone inside a bear trap

items.title: Award-winning artwork revealed as 'blatant copy'

children.content: The Doyles award committee is investigating why it took nearly 12 months to discover a winning landscape painting was an imitation of another artist's work.

image.altText: A photo of an artwork depicting two childlike figures at the beach.

items.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on SA coast

children.content: Two dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, as well as another two wild birds in Western Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

image.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

items.title: Qatar gas complex explosion leaves at least 13 people dead

children.content: The Sunday night blast in Qatar's Ras Laffan gas processing facility is one of the deadliest events recorded in gas industry.

image.altText: The sunsets over power lines and pylons at the Ras Laffan industrial city

items.title: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie is dead, US media reports

children.content: CNN reported that one of the notes claimed that the mother of NBC's Today show host, Savannah Guthrie, was dead, and those who kidnapped her did not mean to kill her, but she died shortly after her disappearance.

image.altText: A photo of Savannah Guthrie alongside her mother Nancy behind a crowd with posters.

items.title: Meet the 'King of the North' set to become the UK's new PM

children.content: A popular mayor but a virtual unknown on the global stage, Andy Burnham is poised to become Sir Keir Starmer's successor. Here's what that could look like.

articles.title: 'They've lost the plot': UK Labour could learn from Rudd-Gillard soap opera

children.content: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

image.altText: Ken Rudd, wearing a dark suit, gestures with his right hand while holding a pen. Julia Gillard is in the background.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

articles.title: More or Less quiz: Which British PMs have lasted the longest?

children.content: Some British PMs have held office across decades. Others have struggled to outlast a lettuce. It's time to test your knowledge.

image.altText: A screenshot from a Daily Star broadcast shows a lettuce with googly eyes next to portrait of Liz Truss

articles.title: Andy Burnham sworn in as MP in hours after Starmer's resignation

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer says he will resign as prime minister of the United Kingdom and spend more time as a husband and father.

image.altText: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer hugs his wife Victoria

items.title: Man fined for selling 19 tonnes of horse meat to greyhound trainers

children.content: A Wagga Wagga man will be more than $40,000 out of pocket after selling tonnes of horse meat to dog trainers in NSW and Victoria.

image.altText: A man with a beard looking at the camera, serious, not smiling.

items.title: Jail for man who sexually assaulted 76-year-old in aged care home

children.content: A 76-year-old woman was left "crying buckets" after being sexually assaulted in a Mid North Coast aged care home, a court hears. Her attacker has now been jailed for 12 years.

image.altText: Man in hoodie on suburban street

items.title: Renewable energy company admits 'failures' caused destruction of Aboriginal site

children.content: ACEREZ introduces strict cultural heritage protocols after a rock shelter was irreparably damaged.

image.altText: A pile of rocks showing a demolished rock shelter

items.title: Government responses criticised two months on from Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Eight weeks on from the alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby, Alice Springs community leaders are calling for an audit to determine why federal and territory government spending does not seem to be improving Indigenous living conditions and safety.

image.altText: A pink flower resting on the ground, colourful candles are burning nearby.

items.title: 'Shaman' lied about magic mushrooms before Adelaide fashion icon's death, inquest hears

children.content: A coronial inquest is investigating the death of Adelaide fashion icon Carol Foord, who used magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment prior to her death in June 2024.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt is photographed outdoors with soft sunlight and greenery in the background.

items.title: Inspired by volcanoes, this new technology could affect billions of people

children.content: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains a billion people depend on.
